name: Old Fall River Road — RMNP
notes: Rocky Mountain NP. One-way unpaved road to Alpine Visitor Center. 11,796 ft. Elk and tundra.

name: Valley of Fire OHV — Overton
notes: Red sandstone formations near Vegas. Stunning desert scenery. Limited OHV access.

name: Ochoco NF — Marks Creek
notes: Central OR. Quiet forest riding east of Prineville. Less crowded than west side.

name: Walker Valley OHV — Burlington
notes: NW Washington. Rocky and muddy PNW riding. North Cascades foothills.

name: Lolo NF — Clearwater Crossing
notes: Near Missoula. Clearwater River area. Beautiful Montana forest riding.

name: Mulberry Gap — Ellijay GA
notes: N Georgia mountains. Mountain bike resort that allows motos. Epic trails + cabin lodging.

name: Turkey Creek OHV — Talladega NF AL
notes: NE Alabama. Talladega NF trails. Rocky and rooty terrain.

name: Brimstone White Knuckle Event — Huntsville TN
notes: Cumberland Plateau. 100+ miles extreme trails. Mountain views. One of the best in the SE.

name: Idaho BDR (IDBDR) — Jarbidge to Montana
notes: Epic BDR from Jarbidge NV to Montana. Magruder Corridor, Lolo Motorway, Burgdorf Hot Springs. Bucket list!

name: Colorado BDR (COBDR) — Durango to Wyoming
notes: Durango to Wyoming. Many 12,000+ ft passes. San Juan Mountains through entire state. Epic.

name: Utah BDR (UTBDR) — Kanab to Bear Lake
notes: Kanab to Bear Lake. Red rock country to alpine mountains. Diverse terrain.

name: Arizona BDR (AZBDR) — Mexico to Utah
notes: Mexican border to Utah. Desert floor to alpine. Rim country, Apache Trail area. Year-round sections.

name: New Mexico BDR (NMBDR) — Mexico to Colorado
notes: Mexican border to CO. Gila NF, Jemez Mountains, Ghost Ranch country. Diverse landscapes.

name: Oregon BDR (ORBDR)
notes: CA border to WA. Cascades and high desert. Volcanic landscapes. Beautiful PNW.

name: Washington BDR (WABDR)
notes: OR border to Canada. Cascades and eastern WA. Alpine passes, volcanoes. Challenging.

name: Nevada BDR (NVBDR)
notes: Border to border through Great Basin. Hot springs, ghost towns, total isolation. Remote.

name: Montana BDR (MTBDR)
notes: Big Sky Country end to end. Dramatic mountain terrain. Grizzly country. Wild Montana.

name: Wyoming BDR (WYBDR)
notes: Through the Bighorns, Absarokas, and Wind Rivers. Vast open spaces and mountain passes.

name: Owyhee Backcountry Byway
notes: SW Idaho. 100+ miles through Owyhee Canyon country. Remote desert canyons and hot springs. Epic.

name: South Fork Boise River — Anderson Ranch
notes: Boise area. Black's Creek Road to Anderson Ranch Reservoir. River canyon riding.

name: Granite High Country — Idaho City
notes: Boise NF. Alpine granite terrain near Idaho City. Technical high-elevation riding.

name: Magruder Corridor — Selway-Bitterroot
notes: 100-mile corridor between 2 wilderness areas. One of the most remote roads in the lower 48. Epic.

name: Burgdorf Hot Springs Area — McCall
notes: Payette NF. Historic hot springs. Remote forest trails north of McCall. IDBDR highlight.

name: Lolo Motorway — Nez Perce Trail
notes: Lewis & Clark and Nez Perce historic route. 100 miles of remote ridge riding. N Idaho. Legendary.

name: Georgia Traverse — AT to coast route
notes: Mountains to coast dual sport route through Georgia. Mix of forest roads and rural highways.

name: Chilhowee Mountain OHV — Tellico Plains
notes: Cherokee NF. Mountain ridge riding. Views of Great Smoky Mountains. SE TN.

name: Mine Made Adventure Trail — Hazard KY
notes: Former coal mine land. 50+ miles. Growing trail system in eastern KY mountains.

name: North Carolina BDR (NCBDR)
notes: TN border to SC border through Blue Ridge Mountains. Appalachian forest roads and gravel.

name: Virginia BDR (VABDR)
notes: Blue Ridge to Appalachian plateau. Shenandoah Valley and mountain forest roads.

name: Mid-Atlantic BDR (MABDR)
notes: Georgia to NY through Appalachian Mountains. 1,050 miles. Epic multi-week ride.

name: Appalachian BDR (APBDR)
notes: Full Appalachian traverse. Alabama to Vermont. Multiple weeks. The ultimate eastern US ride.

name: Shasta-Trinity NF — Scott Mountain
notes: Northern CA. Mt. Shasta views. Remote NorCal forest riding.

name: Lassen NF — Caribou Wilderness Edge
notes: Near Lassen Volcanic NP. Volcanic landscape and alpine forest. Unique terrain.

name: Modoc NF — Devil's Garden
notes: NE California. Lava beds and high desert sage. Most remote NF in CA. Zero crowds.

name: Klamath NF — Salmon River
notes: NW CA wilderness edge. Salmon River country. Old growth forest and mountain trails.

name: Sierra NF — Dinkey Creek
notes: Central Sierra. Granite country with alpine lakes. Less known than Mammoth area.

name: Tahoe NF — Fordyce Creek
notes: Near Truckee. High Sierra granite and forest. Lake Fordyce and alpine scenery.

name: Prescott NF — Lynx Lake Trails
notes: Near Prescott. Pine forest riding at 5,500 ft. Easy access and scenic.

name: Apache Trail — Tonto NF
notes: Historic highway east of Phoenix. Canyon Lake, Tortilla Flat, Fish Creek Hill. Stunning desert canyon.

name: Dragoon Mountains — Cochise Stronghold
notes: SE AZ sky island. Historic Chiricahua Apache territory. Granite spires and desert.

name: Skyline Drive — Manti-La Sal NF
notes: 100-mile ridge road! 10,000+ ft. Views east to desert and west to valleys. One of UT's best.

name: Hole in the Rock Road — Escalante
notes: 57-mile road through Grand Staircase to Lake Powell. Slot canyon access. Historic Mormon route.

name: Mizpah Hotel Area — Tonopah
notes: Central NV mining district. Old mine roads and desert mountains. Stargazing capital.

name: Deschutes NF — Waldo Lake Area
notes: Cascades. One of the clearest lakes in the world. Forest trails and mountain views.

name: Colockum Pass — Ellensburg
notes: Central WA. Mountain pass with Columbia Basin views. Mix of forest and open.

name: Sawtooth Valley — Stanley
notes: Sawtooth Mountains near Stanley. Iconic Idaho peaks. World-class alpine scenery.

name: Flathead NF — Jewel Basin
notes: Near Kalispell. Alpine lakes and mountain riding near Glacier NP. Stunning.

name: Allegheny NF — Marienville OHV
notes: NW Pennsylvania. Only NF in PA. Hardwood forest trails.

name: Green Mountain NF — Vermont
notes: Central Vermont. Mountain forest roads. Fall foliage riding is world-class.

name: Georgia Pass — Breckenridge
notes: 11,598 ft. Easy pass near Breckenridge. Continental Divide. Tenmile Range views.

name: Webster Pass — Montezuma
notes: 12,096 ft pass near Keystone. Ghost town of Montezuma. Steep shelf road sections.

name: Radical Hill — Pitkin County
notes: Near Aspen. High alpine trail with 360° views. Elk Mountains.

name: Kennebec Pass — La Plata Mountains
notes: 11,600 ft. SW Colorado. La Plata Mountains near Durango. Technical rocky sections.

name: Medano Pass — Great Sand Dunes
notes: Access to Great Sand Dunes NP from the east. Deep sand creek crossings. Unique terrain.

name: Rowher Flats OHV — Santa Clarita
notes: Close to LA. Technical single track. Angeles NF. Popular SoCal enduro spot.

name: Drinkwater Flat — Frazier Park
notes: Los Padres NF. Mountain meadows and pine forests above LA. Easy forest roads.

name: Wedge Overlook — San Rafael Swell
notes: Little Grand Canyon overlook. 1,000-ft drop to San Rafael River. Jaw-dropping views.

name: Mexican Mountain — San Rafael Swell
notes: San Rafael Swell. Technical desert riding through slot canyons and mesas.

name: Broken Arrow Trail — Sedona
notes: Iconic Sedona slickrock. Short but stunning. Technical red rock riding.

name: Soldier Trail — Sedona
notes: Sedona single track. Exposed red rock with panoramic views. Technical but rewarding.

name: Chino Valley OHV — Prescott area
notes: North of Prescott. Open BLM desert riding. Easy access.

name: Gila NF — Pinos Altos Range
notes: Silver City area. Gila NF mountain riding. Pinos Altos Range. 9,000+ ft peaks.

name: Rio Grande Del Norte — Taos Plateau
notes: High desert plateau near Taos. Rio Grande Gorge views. Open sagebrush riding.

name: Wind River Range — Lander Area
notes: Shoshone NF. Wind River Range riding near Lander. Some of the best in WY.

name: Cloud Peak Wilderness Edge — Bighorns
notes: Bighorn NF. Ride the edge of Cloud Peak Wilderness. Alpine meadows and 13,000 ft peaks.

name: Bob Marshall Wilderness Edge — Augusta
notes: Lewis & Clark NF. Edge of "The Bob." Montana's most iconic wilderness. Grizzly country.

name: Pioneer Mountains — Wise River
notes: SW Montana. Pioneer Mountains Scenic Byway. 10,000 ft peaks. Hot springs.

name: Salmon River Mountains — Challis
notes: Salmon-Challis NF. River of No Return country. Wild and remote central Idaho.

name: Pisgah NF — Bent Creek
notes: Near Asheville NC. Mountain forest trails. Good mix of difficulty.

name: Cherokee NF — Tellico River
notes: SE TN. Tellico River valley. Cherohala Skyway area. Mountain forest riding.

name: Big Bear — Holcomb Creek
notes: Big Bear area. Creek canyon trails. San Bernardino NF. Popular SoCal spot.

name: Raton Saddle — Gorman
notes: Los Padres NF. Ridge between Castaic and Gorman. Good views. Los Angeles area.

name: Nipton Road BLM — Searchlight NV/CA
notes: CA/NV border. Open desert riding. Near I-15. Good winter riding.

name: Potash Road — Moab
notes: Colorado River canyon road. Petroglyphs and potash mine. Stunning cliff walls.

name: Onion Creek — Moab
notes: Multiple creek crossings. Technical canyon route. Fisher Towers area. Challenging.

name: Notom-Bullfrog Road — Capitol Reef
notes: 70-mile scenic byway through Waterpocket Fold. Capitol Reef backcountry access.

name: Mogollon Rim Road — Payson to Flagstaff
notes: 100+ mile forest road along the Rim. Ponderosa pine. 7,600 ft elevation. Classic AZ ride.

name: General Springs Cabin — Mogollon Rim
notes: Tonto NF. Historic cabin area on the Rim. Forest trails with views off the edge.

name: Woods Canyon Lake — Mogollon Rim
notes: Apache-Sitgreaves NF. Alpine lake on the Rim. Easy forest roads. Family-friendly.

name: Walker Lake — Hawthorne
notes: West central NV. Desert lake riding. Open BLM land around lake.

name: Paradise Peak — Gabbs
notes: Central NV. Desert peak climb. 9,000+ ft summit views. Remote.

name: Boreas Pass — Breckenridge to Como
notes: 11,481 ft. Old railroad grade. Easy pass with 360° views. Historic cabins.

name: Saints John — Montezuma
notes: Ghost town above 11,000 ft near Keystone. Technical climb. Historic mining.

name: Rollins Pass East — Winter Park
notes: Old railroad route. 11,660 ft. Continental Divide. Epic views. Winter Park area.

name: Wilson River — Tillamook
notes: Tillamook SF. Coast Range forest riding. Near the Pacific. Wet PNW trails.

name: Three Sisters Wilderness Edge — Sisters
notes: Deschutes NF. Ride edges of wilderness. Volcanic peaks. Central OR Cascades.

name: Bumping Lake — Chinook Pass
notes: Near Mt. Rainier. Alpine lake and forest trails. William O. Douglas Wilderness edge.

name: Stanley Lake — Sawtooth NRA
notes: Stanley area. Alpine lake with Sawtooth Range backdrop. Stunning scenery.

name: Yankee Fork — Custer
notes: Ghost towns and gold dredge. Salmon-Challis NF. Historic mining area. Scenic byway.

name: Manzano Mountains — Albuquerque
notes: Cibola NF east of Albuquerque. Mountain riding with city views. 10,000+ ft peaks.

name: Shawnee NF — Garden of the Gods
notes: Southern Illinois. Sandstone formations. Forest trails. Ozark foothills.

name: Hoosier NF — Nebo Ridge
notes: Indiana's only NF. Hardwood forest trails. South central Indiana.

name: Caliente Bodfish Road — Kern County
notes: Southern Sierra foothills. Easy desert and scrub riding. Bakersfield area.

name: Azusa Canyon — San Gabriel Mountains
notes: Angeles NF. Canyon riding north of LA. Mountain views.

name: Sand Hollow — Imperial Valley
notes: East of El Centro. Small dune area. Easy desert riding.

name: Cedar Mesa — Bears Ears
notes: Bears Ears National Monument. Ancient ruins and canyons. Remote SE Utah.

name: La Sal Mountains Loop — Moab
notes: Classic Moab area loop. Desert to 10,000+ ft alpine. Aspen groves and mountain meadows.

name: Factory Butte — Hanksville
notes: Remote badlands near Hanksville. Mars-like terrain. Open BLM riding around iconic butte.

name: Black Canyon Trail — New River
notes: 80-mile single track from New River to Prescott. Technical desert and forest. Epic long ride.

name: Florence Junction — Legends of Superior Trail
notes: SE of Phoenix. Rocky technical single track. Sonoran Desert. Challenging.

name: Lake Como Road — Pikes Peak
notes: Pikes Peak access. Highest OHV road in North America to 13,800+ ft! Extreme rocky shelf road.

name: Corkscrew Gulch — Silverton
notes: Connects Silverton to California Gulch. Extremely steep and technical. San Juans. Expert only.

name: Montezuma Basin — Ouray
notes: Above Ouray. 12,000+ ft basin with alpine lakes. San Juan Mountains. Stunning.

name: White Clouds — Ketchum area
notes: White Cloud Mountains. Alpine lakes and granite peaks. Remote central Idaho.

name: Craters of the Moon — Arco BLM
notes: Volcanic lava fields. BLM lands around the national monument. Unique landscape.

name: Nellis Dunes — Las Vegas
notes: NE Las Vegas. Sand dunes near Nellis AFB. Popular Vegas local spot.

name: Wallowa Mountains — Enterprise
notes: NE Oregon. "Alps of Oregon." 10,000 ft peaks and glacial valleys. Remote and stunning.

name: Wenatchee NF — Mission Ridge
notes: Central WA Cascades. Mountain riding above Wenatchee. Eastern slopes.

name: Cabinet Mountains — Libby
notes: NW Montana. Cabinet Mountains Wilderness edge. Grizzly and mountain goat country.

name: Medicine Bow NF — Snowy Range
notes: SE Wyoming. 12,000+ ft peaks. Alpine lakes and tundra. Snowy Range Scenic Byway area.

name: White Sands Missile Range Edge
notes: S NM desert. Ride BLM lands around White Sands. Organ Mountains views. Unique gypsum dunes nearby.

name: Chiricahua NM Edge — Arizona
notes: SE AZ sky island. Coronado NF roads around Chiricahua. Rock formations and desert mountains.

name: Top of the World — Moab
notes: Incredible high-desert mesa riding. 360° views of La Sals, Arches, canyonlands. Moab classic.

name: Mirror Lake Scenic Byway — Evanston WY to Kamas UT
notes: Uinta Mountains. 11,000+ ft pass. Alpine lakes everywhere. One of the best scenic byways in US.

name: Molas Pass — Silverton
notes: Above Silverton. 10,910 ft pass. Grenadier Range and Needle Mountains views. Postcard Colorado.

name: Rubicon Trail — Lake Tahoe
notes: Legendary Lake Tahoe trail. Extremely technical. 4x4 mecca but doable on bikes with skill. Iconic.

name: Going to the Sun Road — Glacier NP
notes: One of the most scenic roads in America. 6,646 ft Logan Pass. Glaciers and alpine. No OHVs but bikes allowed.

name: Beartooth Pass — Montana/Wyoming
notes: Scenic byway from Red Lodge to Cooke City. 10,947 ft pass. Spectacular alpine tundra. World-class.

name: Tioga Pass — Yosemite
notes: Eastern Yosemite access. 9,943 ft pass. Tuolumne Meadows and high Sierra. Stunning scenery.

name: Trail Ridge Road — Rocky Mountain NP
notes: Highest continuous paved road in US. 12,183 ft. Alpine tundra. Elk and marmots. World-famous.

name: Pike's Peak Highway — Colorado Springs
notes: 14,115 ft summit. "America's Mountain." Breathtaking views from the top. Paved but epic.

name: Million Dollar Highway — Ouray to Silverton
notes: US 550. Unbelievable San Juan scenery. Red Mountain Pass 11,018 ft. One of America's best drives.

name: Shafer Trail — Canyonlands
notes: Island in the Sky access. Iconic switchbacks descending canyon wall. White Rim connection. Stunning.

name: Cathedral Valley — Capitol Reef
notes: Remote Capitol Reef backcountry. Monolithic sandstone temples. Little-known NP gem.

name: Gemini Bridges — Moab
notes: Natural rock bridges. Easy ride with incredible views. Moab area. Great for all skill levels.

name: Kokopelli Trail — Moab to Fruita
notes: 142-mile point-to-point. Utah to Colorado. Desert slickrock to alpine. Multi-day epic.

name: Flagstaff Loop — Utah
notes: St. George area. Red rock desert loop. Views of Zion NP. Spectacular scenery. Easy riding.

name: Gooseberry Mesa — Hurricane UT
notes: World-famous slickrock riding. Technical but accessible. Zion views. Bucket list for many.

name: Little Creek Mesa — Moab
notes: High mesa with 360° views. Fisher Towers, La Sals, Arches. Classic Moab desert riding.

name: Steelbender — Moab
notes: Extreme technical trail. Rock ledges and obstacles. For expert riders only. Moab hardcore.

name: Cliffhanger — Moab
notes: Aptly named. Narrow shelf roads and technical ledges. Expert terrain. Moab classic for the brave.

name: Golden Spike — Moab
notes: Classic Moab loop. Portal Trail connection. Varied desert terrain. Popular and accessible.

name: Monitor and Merrimac — Moab
notes: Named for Civil War ironclads. Two massive buttes. Easy loop with stunning desert scenery.

name: Metal Masher — Moab
notes: Technical rocky trail. Challenging obstacles. Portal area. For experienced riders.

name: Porcupine Rim — Moab
notes: High rim trail above Castle Valley. La Sal Mountain views. Classic Moab ridge ride.

name: Sovereign Trail — Moab
notes: Technical single track. Rocks and ledges. Moab Rim area. Challenging and scenic.
